description: System call trace (strace) and source code analysis confirming the silent session disconnect bug in ProFTPD 1.3.9 when FTP command lines exceed PR_DEFAULT_CMD_BUFSZ.

key_syscalls: close(1) = 0  [stdout/control socket WRITE side closed — NO 500 written]
strace_evidence.observation: fd 1 (control socket write) is closed WITHOUT any write() of a 500/421 error response. The server logs 'client sent too-long command, ignoring' and 'FTP session closed' then exits cleanly. The client receives only a TCP EOF with zero response bytes.

step_1.action: When command line fills output buffer (buflen reaches 0) without seeing \n, sets properly_terminated_prev_command=FALSE, sets errno=E2BIG, returns -1. After one successful newline is found but previous command was unterminated, logs 'client sent too-long command, ignoring' and returns -1 (E2BIG) again.
step_1.code: if (saw_newline == FALSE) { properly_terminated_prev_command = FALSE; errno = E2BIG; return -1; } if (properly_terminated_prev_command == FALSE) { properly_terminated_prev_command = TRUE; pr_log_pri(PR_LOG_NOTICE, 'client sent too-long command, ignoring'); errno = E2BIG; return -1; }

step_2.action: On E2BIG from telnet_gets2: increments too_large_count, sleeps pr_timer_usleep(250*1000) = 250ms, checks if too_large_count > 3 (triggers return -1). After 4 consecutive E2BIG returns, returns -1 with NO error response queued.
step_2.code: if (errno == E2BIG) { too_large_count++; pr_timer_usleep(250 * 1000); if (too_large_count > 3) { return -1; } continue; }

step_3.action: pr_cmd_read() returning -1 triggers pr_session_disconnect(NULL, PR_SESS_DISCONNECT_CLIENT_EOF, NULL). This is MISCLASSIFIED as client EOF rather than 'oversized command'. No error response (500 or 421) is sent before disconnecting.
step_3.code: res = pr_cmd_read(&cmd); if (res < 0) { pr_session_disconnect(NULL, PR_SESS_DISCONNECT_CLIENT_EOF, NULL); }

step_4.observation: The ENTIRE code path from oversized command to disconnect never calls pr_response_send() or pr_response_add_err(). The error response chain is empty when pr_session_disconnect fires.
step_4.rfc_959_requirement: RFC 959 Section 5.4: 'The server closes the connection only after receiving an appropriate close request from the user-FTP process, or after a fatal error condition. [...] Any time there is some 5xx-level error, the service must be terminated.' RFC 959 4.2.1: Syntax errors should produce 500 or 501 responses.

buffer_size_analysis.threshold_formula: For command VERB with len(VERB)+1 + len(ARG) + 1 >= 4102 (i.e. the \r fills the last buffer slot), E2BIG fires
buffer_size_analysis.threshold_for_16384_reliable_disconnect: 16384-byte arg causes 4 E2BIG cycles (4 * 4102 = 16408 > 16391), cleanly exceeds too_large_count > 3

empirical_results.USER_4095A: Normal response (501 Login incorrect) — fits in buffer
empirical_results.USER_4096A: Silent drop, connection hangs until client closes (1 E2BIG cycle, then blocks)
empirical_results.USER_16384A: Silent drop with EOF in ~1s (4 E2BIG cycles, too_large_count > 3)
empirical_results.rapid_dos_20_iterations: 20/20 silent drops, avg 36.26s per session tie-up, server survives
